content:
I'd be happy to chat with you and help spark some interest. To address your original code though, the snippet you provided is almost complete, but it's missing the import of the `template` variable which should be a Jinja template object. Here's the corrected version: ```python from jinja2 import Template def render_search_results(query, results): template_str = """

Results for {{ query }}

    {% for result in results %}
  • {{ result.title }}
    • {% endfor %}
""" template = Template(template_str) rendered = template.render(query=query, results=results) return rendered ``` This will return the rendered template as a string. If you have a Jinja Environment set up, you could also use that to render the template, like this: ```python from jinja2 import Environment env = Environment() template = env.from_string(template_str) rendered = template.render(query=query, results=results) ``` This creation of the Environment is useful when working with multiple templates, as it allows you to reuse the Environment across multiple renderings.
